meta.title: BMW S55 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: BMW S55 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW S55 is the high‑performance twin‑turbo straight‑six from the F80 M3 and F82 M4 — a potent track‑bred engine, but crank hub and rod bearing issues are well‑documented. Backed by strong UK enthusiast demand [ESTIMATED].

rows.value: 3 models (M3 F80, M4 F82/F83, M2 Competition F87)

rows.metric: Confidence: 75% confidence — based on extensive owner‑forum data, specialist rebuild reports, and parts‑supply validation [ESTIMATED].

rows.value: 3.5★ × 4 = 14 — a robust turbo engine, but crank‑hub and rod‑bearing issues are real

rows.metric: Reconditioned supply midpoint £9,500 (£7,000–£12,000) → falls in £7,000–£9,999 band

rows.value: Mix of straightforward fixes (oil leaks, sensors) and involved ones (crank‑hub, bearings)

rows.metric: M‑specific parts are widely available but expensive; strong aftermarket support

rows.value: Recon cost (~£9,500) vs conservative £15,000 lowest host value → 63% (60‑74% band)
One-line verdict: The S55 is a potent, track‑ready twin‑turbo six — but crank‑hub and rod‑bearing risks are real — budget for preventative work or a full inspection.

rows.metric: Best for: Track‑day enthusiasts and those seeking a high‑revving M car with strong tuning potential.
rows.value: Avoid if: No documented crank‑hub fix or rod‑bearing inspection above 50,000 miles.

rows.metric: BMW S55 — Where It's Fitted (BMW Models)

rows.value: M4, M4 Competition, M4 CS, M4 GTS
Exclusive to BMW — no cross‑brand fitment.
Labour estimate: Fitting an S55 typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].

rows.metric: CTA line: → Compare BMW S55 engine prices from UK specialists
1. How much does a BMW S55 engine replacement cost?
rows.value: A reconditioned S55 typically costs £7,000–£12,000 supply only [ESTIMATED], with final price varying by specification (standard, Competition, CS) and any included upgrades. Fitting adds £780–£1,300 depending on workshop rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

rows.metric: 2. Is the BMW S55 a reliable engine?
rows.value: The S55 earns 3.5★ out of 5 [ESTIMATED]. It is significantly more robust than its predecessor (S65 V8) but has known weak points: the crank‑hub can spin and rod‑bearings can wear, especially on tuned cars.

rows.metric: 3. What is the most common BMW S55 failure?
rows.value: Crank‑hub spin is the most catastrophic — the timing chain hub can slip, causing valve‑to‑piston contact. Typically occurs on high‑torque, high‑revving or tuned cars from 40,000–80,000 miles. A preventative hub fix costs £1,500–£2,500.

rows.metric: 4. Which BMW models use the S55 engine?
rows.value: The S55 powers the F80 M3 saloon, F82/F83 M4 coupe/convertible, and the F87 M2 Competition. It was also used in the M4 GTS, CS and M2 CS variants.

rows.metric: 5. How long does the BMW S55 engine last?
rows.value: With meticulous maintenance — including 5,000‑mile oil changes and a crank‑hub fix before tuning — an S55 can exceed 150,000 reliable miles. Rod‑bearing wear is less common than on the S65 but should be inspected at 60,000 miles.

rows.metric: Why UK S55 Owners Trust bmwengine.uk
rows.value: * Trusted Suppliers — Every garage and engine rebuilder in our network is vetted, rated and reviewed by real S55 owners, as part of the Engine Finders network.

rows.metric: * Compare Companies — We compare actual quotes from UK specialists so you see real fitted prices — not just advertised supply‑only rates.
rows.value: * UK Coverage — Our specialist network covers England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland, with BMW M‑engine expertise in every region.

rows.metric: Final CTA: Find the best BMW S55 engine replacement UK drivers rely on. Compare real prices from trusted specialists on EnginesMarket today and get back on the road with confidence.
